#+title: acme_bank
#+description: Source data for Acme Bank: a synthetic four-entity investment-bank holding group (Group, UK, US, HK) with LEIs, business units, portfolios, books, and staff accounts, generated by a config-driven Python script.
#+type: dataset_overview
#+dataset_name: acme_bank
#+dataset_version: 1.0
#+dataset_type: crafted
#+source_methodology: Python script (generate_data.py), Faker for GB/US locales, a curated name pool for Hong Kong; see methodology.txt for the full approach and how to extend it.
#+filetags: :acme_bank_holding_group:refdata:sprint_24:
#+created: 2026-07-22
#+updated: 2026-07-22

* Summary

Source data for Acme Bank: four synthetic legal entities (Acme Bank
Group plc, Acme Bank UK plc, Acme Bank US Inc, Acme Bank HK Ltd),
each with its own LEI, business units (trading desks + support
functions), portfolios, books, and staff accounts. This is task 1 of
the Acme provisioning architecture (see [[id:CB7351E9-8E7E-4BA4-9399-4EB22F6192D4][Commission Acme Bank]]'s =*
Decisions=): JSON only — no SQL, no server-side wiring yet.

* Contents

| File                     | Description                                                       |
|--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| =generate_data.py=        | Config-driven generator; re-run to regenerate all JSON below       |
| =companies.json=          | The 4 legal entities (reference/documentation)                     |
| =lei_entities.json=       | ores_dq_lei_entities_artefact_tbl shape — checksum-valid 9695 LEIs  |
| =lei_relationships.json=  | ores_dq_lei_relationships_artefact_tbl shape — parent/subsidiary    |
| =business_units.json=     | ores_dq_business_units_artefact_tbl shape — desks + support funcs   |
| =portfolios.json=         | ores_dq_portfolios_artefact_tbl shape — one per desk + virtual roll-up |
| =books.json=              | ores_dq_books_artefact_tbl shape — one book per desk portfolio     |
| =accounts.json=           | New shape (no artefact table yet) — 45 staff accounts, one fixed password |
| =manifest.json=           | Dataset/catalog descriptor, following the =slovaris= convention    |
| =methodology.txt=         | Full generation approach and how to extend (add an office/desk)    |

* Why this dataset

Unlike [[id:1161B5B0-3D67-4215-A6A0-093618B2FE3E][slovaris]] (a self-contained fictional universe), Acme Bank is
designed to integrate with the system's real reference data — its
LEIs are checksum-valid on GLEIF's reserved =9695= test prefix so it
fits the same import machinery real tenants use, and it's meant to
trade against real GLEIF-imported counterparties. Each of the four
legal entities needs its own independent organisational structure
(existing publish-from-dq functions are single-party-scoped per
call), which is why every business-unit/portfolio/book/account
record carries a =company_code= tag.

* See also

- [[id:CB7351E9-8E7E-4BA4-9399-4EB22F6192D4][Commission Acme Bank: a realistic holding-group test entity]] — the parent story; see its =* Decisions= for the full 4-task provisioning architecture.
- [[id:69E9CD42-EED4-403F-9331-AE369B8581B6][Analyse Acme Bank corporate structure and current populatability]] — the earlier analysis this dataset implements.
- [[id:1161B5B0-3D67-4215-A6A0-093618B2FE3E][slovaris]] — contrast case: a self-contained fictional world, deliberately *not* the pattern Acme follows.
